macro_segments.title: Introduction to the Masechet and Overview of Sanctified Fields

micro_segments.title: Overview of Masechet Arachin and its connection to Parshat Bechukotai
The structure of the masechet focusing on hakdashot (donations) to the Beit HaMikdash

macro_segments.title: The Debate of Rav and Shmuel on Sanctifying During the Yovel Year Itself

micro_segments.title: The original Mishnah ruling on sanctifying fields and the position of Rav and Shmuel on deduction and full payment
Baraita stating that sanctification cannot occur during the Yovel year itself
Rav's response using the hermeneutic principle of Rabbi that endpoints are inclusive
Gemara questioning how many years remain if sanctification begins on the Yovel year itself
Resolution that Rabbi holds like Rabbi Yehuda that the Yovel year is year one of the next cycle

macro_segments.title: The Rule Against Redemption in Partial Years and the Principle of Whole Year Units

micro_segments.title: Baraita on the inability to redeem in units less than a full year after the Yovel
Gemara explaining that this rule means you only count full years and round up for the benefit of hekdesh
Tannaic teaching deriving the rule from the verse about calculating in remaining years
Explanation of how months can be counted when it benefits hekdesh by rounding down

macro_segments.title: The Measure of a Field: the Chomer of Barley Seed and Treatment of Non-Plantable Areas

micro_segments.title: Mishna on the value of a field based on the amount of barley seed it can be planted with
Mishna on deep crevices and tall rocks that do not count toward field acreage
Baraita specifying that the measure is seed amount not harvest amount and hand-spread not ox-spread
Gemara discussing why rocks and crevices are excluded and how smaller non-plantable features are handled

macro_segments.title: Deduction Based on Remaining Years and the Inability to Accept Installment Plans

micro_segments.title: Baraita teaching that deduction applies even when hekdesh has possessed the field
Mishna forbidding owners from making installment payments when redeeming sanctified fields
Distinction between owners and others redeeming: owners must add a fifth, others do not

macro_segments.title: Redemption and Reversion at Yovel: Who Can Redeem and Consequences

micro_segments.title: Mishna scenario where the owner redeems: field stays with owner
Mishna scenario where son redeems: field reverts to father at Yovel
Mishna scenario where other person redeems: field lost to owner at Yovel
Mishna scenario where a priest redeems: field divided among all priests

macro_segments.title: Baraita Clarifying the Difference Between Son and Other Redeemers

micro_segments.title: Baraita teaching that a field sold to someone other than the son does not return to the owner
Gemara debating whether brother should be included like son based on the concept of continuity
Gemara conclusion that son has greater status than brother based on yibbum principles

macro_segments.title: Unresolved Questions About Daughter and Wife Redeeming the Field

micro_segments.title: Rava bar Avuha asking whether a daughter can preserve the field for the father
Gemara discussion of whether daughter should be treated like son due to being exempt from yibbum
Gemara concluding that daughter is like other via inheritance hierarchy rules
Rami bar Chama asking who represents continuity for a woman who sanctified: husband or son
Gemara leaving unresolved the question of husband versus son for woman's sanctified field

macro_segments.title: Sanctification Within Two Years of Yovel and the Case of a Priest's Possession

micro_segments.title: Rami bar Chama asking Rav Chisda whether a field sanctified less than two years before Yovel goes to priests
Rav Chisda explaining that any field that can be redeemed goes to priests if not redeemed
Baraita teaching that if a priest redeems the field, it cannot stay as his private possession
Baraita explaining the principle that only true inheritance stays with the priest owner
Preview of next Mishna on what happens when nobody redeems and field remains sanctified

topical_tags.term: Field goes to priests at Yovel
